title: Performance Optimization Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of Redis caching, Gzip compression, and using Clinic.js to visualize CPU/memory bottlenecks.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily goal of 'Performance Optimization' in a Node.js application?
options: Reducing the time it takes for your server to process requests and send back data to your users, especially at high scale.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which tool would you use to store the result of a slow database query in RAM for near-instant access next time?
Response compression (like Gzip or Brotli) can often shrink the size of your server's HTML/JSON data by up to ________%.
Which and/or what was the primarily benefit of using a 'profiler' tool like Clinic.js?
options: To automatically write your code for you.
To visualize bottlenecks in your server's CPU and memory usage to identify slow code or memory leaks.

questions.question: Does following the 'Asynchronous' (non-blocking) pattern help Node.js achieve high performance compared to traditional synchronous servers?
options: Yes, as it allows the server to keep processing other tasks while waiting for I/O (like a database query) to finish.
